briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 10, 2026.

sections.title: ⚠️ Severe Domestic and International Impacts and Vulnerabilities of Climate Change
sections.content: According to news released today, there was a direct report that Gyeonggi Province residents are suffering due to climate change [1]. Internationally, research results were also announced indicating that climate change is amplifying hurricane floods and deaths [19], and, in conjunction with rapid development, is causing landslides [22]. In particular, Korea's kimchi industry is facing pressure from climate change, in addition to low-cost imported products [24], and in marine ecosystems, concerns are being raised that climate change is globally contributing to the increased mortality of gray whales [18]. Meanwhile, a surge in Antarctic tourism is putting pressure on the vulnerable polar ecosystem and raising global awareness of climate change [23].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Agricultural Sector Responses
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts are continuing to achieve carbon neutrality. The Cheongju Carbon Neutrality Support Center successfully led citizen participation in the Green Festival [3], and Seogwipo City is expanding its leading villages for carbon neutrality practices [4]. In Dangjin, the continuity of industry, settlement, and carbon neutrality was emphasized [5], and the Dongdaemun-gu Facilities Management Corporation signed a business agreement with the Carbon Neutrality Support Center, strengthening the community's carbon neutrality capabilities [6]. Particularly in the agricultural sector, Geumsan County is supporting ginseng production materials for climate change response [2], and in Jeju, a case was reported where carbon neutrality is being spearheaded by shredding citrus pruning branches instead of burning them [9]. Imsil County is attempting to address both rural labor shortages and carbon neutrality simultaneously by disseminating dry paddy harrowing technology [10], and the Yeoncheon County Carbon Neutrality Experience Booth was also successfully operated [8]. As such, positive movements for eco-friendly practices and carbon neutrality are spreading across local communities and industries [16].

sections.title: 🌍 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Green Hydrogen Economy
sections.content: As the transition to renewable energy accelerates globally, a report by the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) officially confirmed that solar and wind power generation and battery storage systems are cheaper and more stable than fossil fuels [13]. Amidst this trend, Serbia has emerged as a leader in energy transition, officially launching its green hydrogen economy with the goal of becoming a hydrogen hub in the Balkan region [12]. Domestically, political interest in expanding renewable energy and achieving carbon neutrality is also growing, with discussions on carbon neutrality policies by figures such as Gunpo mayoral preliminary candidate Han Dae-hee [7].

sections.title: 🔴 Structural Limitations and Calls for Improvement in Climate Policy Governance
sections.content: Despite policy efforts to address climate change, concerns are being raised regarding the structural limitations of governance. According to a report by the National Assembly Futures Institute, Korea's energy policy direction changes drastically with government transitions, undermining policy consistency, thus emphasizing the need for scientific and long-term planning [15]. Internationally, structural limitations of climate governance overlooking the oceans were revealed at COP30 [21], and regressive policy decisions, such as the signing of bills to halt climate change efforts in some regions [20], are also emerging. This once again reminds us of the importance of consistent and comprehensive policy formulation and international cooperation in responding to the climate crisis.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the realistic impacts of climate change, along with diverse domestic and international response efforts, and policy challenges. A consistent and scientific approach for a sustainable future is more critical than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: 10일 국회미래연구원이 발간한 보고서에 따르면, 한국의 에너지 정책 방향이 정권 교체에 따라 급격히 변화하며 과도한 목표 설정, 목표 미달, 목표치 하향 조정이 반복되어 정책 일관성이 훼손되었다.
summary.outlook: 국회미래연구원은 과학적 기반의 에너지 계획 수립을 제언하며, 향후 에너지 정책의 일관성 확보가 필요함을 시사한다.
